steps.title: Identify the standard circle equation
steps.content:
The standard equation of a circle with center $(h, k)$ and radius $r$ is given by the formula below.

From the problem, the center is at $(-8, 6)$ and the radius is 5. Therefore:
  • $h = -8$
  • $k = 6$
  • $r^2 = 5^2 = 25$

steps.title: Substitute and simplify
steps.content:
Substitute the center coordinates and radius-squared into the standard form:
$$(x - (-8))^2 + (y - 6)^2 = 25$$
Simplify the double negative in the first term:
$$(x + 8)^2 + (y - 6)^2 = 25$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
